mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting conditions and lower hurdle rates for equities, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when rate cuts are underway.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% indicates restrictive policy that pressures valuations and financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations, or if the threshold adds no lift versus unconditional baseline performance.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth marks a deteriorating labor market, which weakens consumption expectations and raises recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal does not outperform either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in the majority of episodes with rising UNRATE and weakening PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth crosses above its recent trend, retail demand strength signals better near-term corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than negative-growth episodes, or if the sign of the effect flips in the same expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage across cyclicals, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when production momentum is strengthening.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not associated with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses significance in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between HOUST and GDPC1 captures housing-led demand acceleration; when housing starts rise while real GDP is expanding, equity returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days because cyclically sensitive earnings expectations improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-GDPC1 signal does not beat either proxy alone, or if positive joint readings fail to produce higher 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market stress and imminent demand weakening, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice recession risk quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE jump episodes are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect is absent after controlling for the recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-hike stabilization phase, CPIAUCSL cooling together with unchanged or lower FEDFUNDS reduces real-rate pressure and supports multiple expansion, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation combined with flat or falling FEDFUNDS does not improve 60-day SPX returns versus other macro states, or if the interaction effect is not statistically distinct from either series alone.
